item.ptxt: When DTC P0300, P0301, P0302, P0303 or P0304 is detected, MIL blinks or lights up according to detecting condition. For details, refer to DTC P0300 / P0301 / P0302 / P0303 / P0304 .
With the CAN communication generic scan tool, only DTC marked with asterisk (*) in the following table can be read. (Euro-OBD model)
DTC with asterisk 1 (*1) in the table below is applicable to non-Euro-OBD model.
DTC with asterisk 2 (*2) in the table below is applicable to Euro-OBD model.

entry.ptxt: “A” Camshaft Position Timing Over Advanced or System Performance
Measured CMP does not reach target CMP for specified time.
HO2S-1 heater control circuit is open.
HO2S-1 heater control circuit is shorted to ground.
HO2S-1 heater control circuit is shorted to power supply.
HO2S-2 heater control circuit is open.
HO2S-2 heater control circuit is shorted to ground.
HO2S-2 heater control circuit is shorted to power supply.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit Range / Performance
Measured MAF sensor signal is out of specified range.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit Low
Output voltage of MAF sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.2 V for 5 sec.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit High
Output voltage of MAF sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.8 V for 5 sec.
Intake Air Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Low
Output voltage of IAT sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.1 V for 5 sec.
Intake Air Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit High
Output voltage of IAT sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.9 V for 5 sec.
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Range / Performance
Difference between measured ECT and estimated ECT is more than specified value for specified time with engine running.
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Low
Output voltage of ECT sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.1 V for 5 sec.
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit High
Output voltage of ECT sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.9 V for 5 sec.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit Low
Output voltage of TP sensor (main) signal circuit is lower than 0.2 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit High
Output voltage of TP sensor (main) signal circuit is higher than 4.8 V.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Variation of HO2S-1 signal is higher than 0.5 V.
HO2S-1 signal voltage is higher than 0.6 V for 15 sec. even if HO2S-2 signal voltage is between 0.06 V and 0.42 V.
HO2S-1 signal voltage is lower than 0.1 V for 15 sec. even if HO2S-2 signal voltage is between 0.5 V and 1.08 V.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of HO2S-1 circuit stays lower than 0.06 V from cold engine start to engine warmed up.
Output voltage of HO2S-2 circuit is higher than 0.6 V and output voltage of HO2S-1 circuit is lower than 0.06 V for 15 sec.

entry.ptxt: Output voltage of HO2S-1 circuit is higher than 1.08 V for 5 sec.
Cycle duration from rich to lean and lean to rich of HO2S-1 is longer than specified time.
O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Sensor 1)
Output voltage of HO2S-1 circuit stays between 0.42 and 0.50 V for 15 sec.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of HO2S-2 circuit is lower than 0.09 V for 30 sec. after warming up.
Output voltage of HO2S-2 circuit stays lower than 0.09 V from cold engine start to engine warmed up.

entry.ptxt: Output voltage of HO2S-2 signal circuit is higher than 1.08 V for 5 sec.
O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Sensor 2)
Output voltage of HO2S-2 circuit stays between 0.42 to 0.50 V for 500 sec.
Fuel trim correction is higher than specified value for specified time under specified vehicle condition.
Fuel trim correction is lower than specified value for specified time under specified vehicle condition.
Fuel injector No.1 and/or its circuit is open with engine running.
Fuel injector No.2 and/or its circuit is open with engine running.
Fuel injector No.3 and/or its circuit is open with engine running.
Fuel injector No.4 and/or its circuit is open with engine running.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “B” Circuit Low
Output voltage of TP sensor (sub) signal circuit is lower than 0.2 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “B” Circuit High
Output voltage of TP sensor (sub) signal circuit is higher than 4.8 V.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.1 is lower than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.1 is higher than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.2 is lower than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.2 is higher than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.3 is lower than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.3 is higher than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.4 is lower than specified value with engine running.
Drive circuit voltage of fuel injector No.4 is higher than specified value with engine running.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Misfire, which causes catalyst to overheat during 200 engine revolutions, is detected at 2 or more cylinders. (MIL blinks as long as misfire lasts.)
Misfire, which affects exhaust emission adversely during 1000 engine revolutions, is detected at 2 or more cylinders.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Misfire, which causes catalyst to overheat during 200 engine revolutions, is detected at specific cylinder. (MIL blinks as long as misfire lasts.)
Misfire, which affects exhaust emission adversely during 1000 engine revolutions, is detected at specific cylinder.

entry.ptxt: Knock sensor signal is out of specified range for 4 sec.

item.ptxt: This DTC is detected if any one of the following error counters related to CKP sensor signal is more than specified number with engine running.
Displaced gap by one tooth or more
Detection of gap at missing gap range
Faulty distance between gaps
CKP sensor signal is not inputted while CMP sensor signal is being inputted.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: CMP sensor signal is not inputted while CKP sensor signal is being inputted.
CMP sensor signal pattern is not equal to the expected pattern.

entry.ptxt: Ignition coil assembly No.1 and/or its circuit malfunction is detected with engine running.
Ignition coil assembly No.2 and/or its circuit malfunction is detected with engine running.
Ignition coil assembly No.3 and/or its circuit malfunction is detected with engine running.
Ignition coil assembly No.4 and/or its circuit malfunction is detected with engine running.
Average amplitude of HO2S-2 signal is more than specified value for 60 sec. under specified vehicle condition.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit
EVAP canister purge valve control circuit is open.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit Low
EVAP canister purge valve control circuit is shorted to ground.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit High
EVAP canister purge valve control circuit is shorted to power supply.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.1 drive circuit is shorted to ground or open.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.2 and/or No.3 drive circuit is shorted to ground or open.
Vehicle speed is slower than 8 km/h (5 mile/h) for 5 sec. at fuel cut.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of A/C refrigerant pressure sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.15 V.
Output voltage of A/C refrigerant pressure sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.93 V.

entry.ptxt: Main power supply voltage is lower than 2.54 V for 10 sec.
Main power supply circuit voltage is lower than 9.8 V for 5 sec. with engine speed 2,200 rpm or more.
Main power supply circuit voltage is higher than 16.5 V for 5 sec. with engine speed 2,200 rpm or more.
Internal failure in ECM is detected.
Fuel pump relay drive circuit is open.
Fuel pump relay drive circuit is shorted to ground.
Fuel pump relay drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
A/C compressor relay drive circuit is open.
A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit Low
A/C compressor relay drive circuit is shorted to ground.
A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit High
A/C compressor relay drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.1 drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.2 and/or No.3 drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
Immobilizer ID and/or Password Not Registered

item.ptxt: Ignition switch model:
Keyless push start model:

entry.ptxt: “A” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Low
OCV control circuit is shorted to ground.
“A” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit High
OCV control circuit is shorted to power supply.
Throttle Actuator “A” Control Motor Circuit / Open
Throttle actuator and/or its circuit malfunction (open, short to power supply or short to ground) is detected.
Throttle Actuator “A” Control Motor Circuit Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Difference between measured throttle valve position and targeted throttle valve position is more than specified value.
Duty ratio of throttle actuator signal is out of specified range for specified time.

entry.ptxt: Throttle Actuator Control Throttle Body Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Time taken by throttle valve to return from partially open position to mechanical default position is more than specified time during return spring check process after setting ignition “OFF”.
Measured default throttle position is out of specified range.

entry.ptxt: Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” Circuit Low
Output voltage of APP sensor (main) signal circuit is lower than 0.6 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” Circuit High
Output voltage of APP sensor (main) signal circuit is higher than 4.4 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “E” Circuit Low
Output voltage of APP sensor (sub) signal circuit is lower than 0.3 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “E” Circuit High
Output voltage of APP sensor (sub) signal circuit is higher than 2.5 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A”/”B” Voltage Correlation
Difference between TP sensor (main) signal voltage and TP sensor (sub) signal voltage is higher than specified value.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D”/”E” Voltage Correlation
Difference between half of APP (main) sensor signal and APP (sub) sensor signal is more than specified value.
Throttle Actuator Control System - Idle Position Not Learned
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: TP sensor (main) or TP sensor (sub) signal voltage at default position is out of specified range for specified time.
Electric throttle control system calibration is not completed.

entry.ptxt: Output voltage of barometric pressure sensor signal is lower than 0.2 V for 5 sec.
Output voltage of barometric pressure sensor signal is higher than 4.8 V for 5 sec.
O2 Sensor Signal Biased / Stuck Lean (Sensor 2)
HO2S-2 signal stays in lean state for 70 sec. with specified driving condition even if A/F is enrichment control.
O2 Sensor Signal Biased / Stuck Rich (Sensor 2)
Output voltage of HO2S-2 signal circuit is higher than 0.25 V for 10 sec. even if vehicle is fuel cut driving condition.
Ignition Coil “A” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition coil assembly No.1 drive circuit voltage is lower than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “A” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition coil assembly No.1 drive circuit voltage is higher than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “B” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition coil assembly No.2 drive circuit voltage is lower than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “B” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition coil assembly No.2 drive circuit voltage is higher than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “C” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition coil assembly No.3 drive circuit voltage is lower than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “C” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition coil assembly No.3 drive circuit voltage is higher than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “D” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition coil assembly No.4 drive circuit voltage is lower than specified value with engine running.
Ignition Coil “D” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition coil assembly No.4 drive circuit voltage is higher than specified value with engine running.
Refer to CAN DTC (Lost Communication and Communication Bus Off) Table .
Lost Communication With ESP® Control Module
Lost Communication With Power Steering Control Module
Lost Communication With Body Control Module
Lost Communication With Body Control Module “keyless start”

servinfosub.title: For A/T Model (Euro-OBD Model)
servinfosub.ptxt: When CAN communication OBD generic scan tool is used, not only ECM-related DTC(s) but also any of the following TCM-related DTCs will be simultaneously displayed on the scan tool.

entry.ptxt: Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Malfunction (P R N D L Input)
Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor “A” Circuit Range / Performance
Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor “A” Circuit Low
Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor “A” Circuit High
Input / Turbine Speed Sensor “A” Circuit No Signal
Output Speed Sensor Circuit No Signal
Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Performance / Stuck Off
Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Stuck On
Shift Solenoid-A Performance or Stuck Off
Shift Solenoid-B Performance or Stuck Off
Pressure Control Solenoid “A” Control Circuit Range / Performance
Pressure Control Solenoid “A” Control Circuit Low
Pressure Control Solenoid “A” Control Circuit High
Internal Control Module Memory Check Sum Error
Torque Request Communication Error from TCM
Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Range / Performance
Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit High
Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Low
